tluaGotoIntoLocalScope.tlua(3,5): error TS100026: Cannot jump into the scope of local 'x'.
tluaGotoIntoLocalScope.tlua(6,12): error TS2454: Variable 'x' is used before being assigned.
tluaGotoIntoLocalScope.tlua(11,5): error TS100026: Cannot jump into the scope of local 'helper'.
tluaGotoIntoLocalScope.tlua(39,5): error TS100026: Cannot jump into the scope of local 'a'.
tluaGotoIntoLocalScope.tlua(42,12): error TS2454: Variable 'b' is used before being assigned.


==== tluaGotoIntoLocalScope.tlua (5 errors) ====
    // Error: `x` is still in scope at ::done::, so the jump enters its scope.
    function intoScope(): number
        goto done
        ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS100026: Cannot jump into the scope of local 'x'.
        local x = 1;
        ::done::
        return x;
               ~
!!! error TS2454: Variable 'x' is used before being assigned.
    end
    
    // Error: a `local function` declares a local too.
    function intoFunctionScope(): void
        goto done
        ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS100026: Cannot jump into the scope of local 'helper'.
        local function helper(): void
        end
        ::done::
        helper();
    end
    
    // Accepted: the label sits in the block's trailing void region, where no local
    // is in scope. This is the relaxation that makes the skip-to-end idiom legal.
    function trailingLabel(): void
        goto done
        local x = 1;
        ::done::
    end
    
    // Accepted: a local inside a nested block died with that block.
    function subBlockLocal(): void
        goto done
        do
            local x = 1;
        end
        ::done::
        return;
    end
    
    // Error: a multi-name local declares every binding at once, so jumping past it
    // enters their scope. The message names the first binding.
    function intoMultiNameScope(): number
        goto done
        ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS100026: Cannot jump into the scope of local 'a'.
        local a, b = 1, 2;
        ::done::
        return b;
               ~
!!! error TS2454: Variable 'b' is used before being assigned.
    end
    
    // Accepted: a backward jump leaves scopes, it never enters one.
    function backwardPastLocal(): void
        ::top::
        local x = 1;
        if x < 5 then goto top end
    end
    
    // Accepted: the loop body's trailing label, jumped to past the body's local.
    function skipToEnd(n: number): number
        local total = 0;
        while n > 0 do
            n = n - 1;
            if n == 3 then goto next end
            local step = n * 2;
            total = total + step;
            ::next::
        end
        return total;
    end
